name: Streusel Plum Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Halved plums or other fruit
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 8-inch square cake pan, 9-inch pie plate, or springform pan.
2. Beat the egg white in a small bowl and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, cream together the margarine, sugar, and egg yolk until fluffy.
4. Combine flour and baking powder, then beat into the egg mixture alternately with milk. Fold in the beaten egg white.
5. Turn the batter into the prepared pan.
6. Halve plums (or slice peach, apple, pear, or nectarine) and arrange over top.
7. For streusel topping: In a small bowl, combine brown sugar, margarine, and cinnamon. Mix well and sprinkle over fruit.
8. Bake at 350°F for about 35 minutes, or until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted into the cake comes out clean. (Time may vary depending on the thickness of the batter.)
9. For glaze: Combine icing sugar, milk, and vanilla in a bowl. Mix well and drizzle over the cake.
My notes: The original recipe halved makes 5 servings, but it tasted so good warm that we only cut out 4 servings. Warmed up in microwave for the second meal. Use extra fruit (fresh or canned) to make 6 servings. This would be nice served at a brunch - moist but not too sweet. Tested with fresh nectarines!
